methods.description: Process for obtaining, dating, storing, cutting, and preparing the Sequoia sempervirens sample while ensuring magnetic cleanliness for subsequent measurements.

steps.description: A sample of the Sequoia sempervirens (m26 NE3-NE5) was obtained from professor Malcom Hughes on December 17, 1999, in the Laboratory of Tree-ring research, University of Arizona.
steps.searchable_summary: obtain tree ring sample
inputs: location: Laboratory of Tree-ring research, University of Arizona

steps.description: The tree sample was collected in Mountain Home State Forest, California (SE of Fresno), and dated by Rex K. Adams.
steps.searchable_summary: record sample collection location and initial dating

steps.description: The specimen was cross-dated for the time interval between 950-1450 years.
steps.searchable_summary: perform cross-dating on tree ring sample

steps.description: The rest of the years, estimated based on Figure 1, is not cross-dated and may be associated with some errors (+/-5 years). In Figure 1, one pinprick (blue dot) indicates the 10th year, two pinpricks in a vertical alignment indicate the 50th year, three pinpricks in a vertical alignment indicate the 100th year, and four pinpricks in a vertical alignment indicate the 1000th year.
steps.searchable_summary: estimate tree ring dates for non-cross-dated sections using pinprick markers

steps.description: Wood was collected in year 1998 and stored for one year in Laboratory of Tree Ring Research, Arizona.
steps.searchable_summary: store wood sample

steps.description: Samples were cut by handheld non-magnetic saw in Pruhonice Paleomagnetic Laboratory (PPL), Czech Republic.
steps.searchable_summary: cut samples using non-magnetic saw

steps.description: During this time samples were kept in a dry box at GSFC and in relatively dry storage facility of PPL to avoid moisture exposure.
steps.searchable_summary: store cut samples in dry conditions

steps.description: During the process of measurement all parts of the sample holder were cleaned with ethyl alcohol and distilled water to insure absence of magnetic contamination.
steps.searchable_summary: clean sample holders to prevent magnetic contamination

steps.description: The NASA specimen was cut into a rod, about 600 mm long, with 100 mm 2 cross-section.
steps.searchable_summary: cut sample into a rod shape

steps.description: A sharp scalpel was used to dissect the wooden rod into cubical samples (sizẽ 1 cm).
steps.searchable_summary: dissect sample rod into cubical samples

steps.description: Small sample (5 mm × 5 mm × 10 mm) from the section near 650 years was cut, using a knife, to fit inside the plastic straw, the holder for Quantum Design MPMS cryogenic magnetometer.
steps.searchable_summary: cut small sample for low-temperature measurements

methods.description: Procedure for counting tree rings within cubical samples to determine tree ring density.

steps.description: Tree rings were counted within each cube to obtain tree ring density ( Figure 2).
steps.searchable_summary: count tree rings within cubical samples

steps.description: We estimated that for every 15 tree rings we may have missed or added an extra ring. This allows obtaining a signal-to-noise ratio value of 15/1.
steps.searchable_summary: estimate signal-to-noise ratio for tree ring density

methods.description: Measurement of high-field magnetic susceptibility using a Vibrating Sample Magnetometer (VSM) at GSFC/NASA.

steps.description: High-field magnetic susceptibilities ( Figure 2) were obtained at GSFC/NASA from the magnetization change between 1 and 2 Tesla field inside the Vibrating Sample Magnetometer (VSM Model 7300 by Lake Shore, 10 times averaging).
steps.searchable_summary: measure high-field magnetic susceptibility using VSM

steps.description: 10 times averaging
steps.searchable_summary: average VSM measurements

steps.description: The signal-to-noise ratio of this value was estimated to be 12/1 based on repetitive measurements of several samples.
steps.searchable_summary: estimate signal-to-noise ratio for high-field susceptibility

steps.description: Each time the empty holder was measured for the final sample correction.
steps.searchable_summary: measure empty sample holder for correction

steps.description: Apply final sample correction using empty holder measurement.
steps.searchable_summary: apply empty holder correction to susceptibility data

methods.description: Measurement of low-field magnetic susceptibility using a KLY-2 Kappabridge at PPL.

steps.description: Low-field magnetic susceptibilities were obtained from the sister samples sent to PPL along with measurements of Natural Remanent Magnetization (NRM), Saturation Isothermal Remanent Magnetization (SIRM), and thermal/alternating field magnetic stability.
steps.searchable_summary: measure low-field magnetic susceptibility using KLY-2 Kappabridge

methods.description: Measurement of Natural Remanent Magnetization (NRM) using a JR-5A Spinner Magnetometer at PPL.

steps.description: The PPL obtained NRM and SIRM from sister samples ( Figure 4) using JR-5A Spinner Magnetometer (measuring range from 2.4 e-3 to 1.6 e+3 mA/m).
steps.searchable_summary: measure natural remanent magnetization using spinner magnetometer

steps.description: All magnetic remanence measurements were done such that samples were kept oriented in respect to each other.
steps.searchable_summary: maintain sample orientation during remanence measurements

methods.method_name: Saturation Isothermal Remanent Magnetization (SIRM) Acquisition and Measurement (PPL)
methods.description: Acquisition of Saturation Isothermal Remanent Magnetization (SIRM) using an electromagnet and measurement using a JR-5A Spinner Magnetometer at PPL.

steps.description: SIRM was acquired in an electromagnet (Polytechnik, Germany) using a direct magnetic field to the state of saturation at the maximum field intensity of 1 T.
steps.searchable_summary: acquire saturation isothermal remanent magnetization

steps.description: The PPL obtained NRM and SIRM from sister samples ( Figure 4) using JR-5A Spinner Magnetometer (measuring range from 2.4 e-3 to 1.6 e+3 mA/m).
steps.searchable_summary: measure saturation isothermal remanent magnetization using spinner magnetometer

steps.description: All magnetic remanence measurements were done such that samples were kept oriented in respect to each other.
steps.searchable_summary: maintain sample orientation during remanence measurements

methods.description: Measurement of magnetic properties at low temperatures using a Quantum Design MPMS cryogenic magnetometer at the Institute for Rock Magnetism (IRM), University of Minnesota.

steps.description: Small sample (5 mm × 5 mm × 10 mm) from the section near 650 years was cut, using a knife, to fit inside the plastic straw, the holder for Quantum Design MPMS cryogenic magnetometer.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare small sample and holder for MPMS

steps.description: The sample was brought to 20 K
steps.searchable_summary: cool sample to 20 K

steps.description: acquired saturation remanence in field of 5T
steps.searchable_summary: acquire saturation remanence at 20 K in 5T field

steps.description: and warmed up to 300 K.
steps.searchable_summary: warm sample to 300 K

steps.description: At 300 K the sample was again given saturation remanence
steps.searchable_summary: acquire saturation remanence at 300 K

steps.description: and brought back to 20 K (see Figure 3).
steps.searchable_summary: cool sample back to 20 K

steps.description: Measure magnetic data during the temperature cycle (20K -> 300K -> 20K) and SIRM acquisition steps.
steps.searchable_summary: measure magnetic moment during low-temperature cycle

methods.description: Partial thermal demagnetization of selected SIRM samples using MAVACS and measurement of magnetic susceptibility after each step using KLY-2 Kappabridge at PPL.

steps.description: SIRM of 12 selected samples were partially thermally demagnetized ( Figure 5) with MAVACS (Magnetic Vacuum Control System).
steps.searchable_summary: perform partial thermal demagnetization on SIRM samples

steps.description: This instrument creates a magnetic vacuum less than 1 nT [10].
steps.searchable_summary: ensure magnetic vacuum during thermal demagnetization

steps.description: Each demagnetization step was followed by measurement of magnetic susceptibility (frequency 875 Hz, field intensity 300 A/m) with KLY-2 Kappabridge [9].
steps.searchable_summary: measure magnetic susceptibility after each thermal demagnetization step

steps.description: Changes in magnetic susceptibility indicate chemical changes within the sample during the heating (see Figure 5).
steps.searchable_summary: analyze changes in magnetic susceptibility to infer chemical changes

steps.description: All magnetic remanence measurements were done such that samples were kept oriented in respect to each other.
steps.searchable_summary: maintain sample orientation during measurements

methods.method_name: Alternating Field (AF) Demagnetization and Stepwise Magnetization (PPL)
methods.description: Demagnetization of selected SIRM samples by alternating field and subsequent stepwise magnetization at PPL.

steps.description: Five samples with SIRM from the older section of the tree (600-1000 years A.D.) were demagnetized by alternating field up to 0.1 T with instrument Schonstedt GSD-1 demagnetizer
steps.searchable_summary: perform alternating field demagnetization on SIRM samples

steps.description: and subsequently stepwise magnetized by field up to 0.5 T.
steps.searchable_summary: perform stepwise magnetization on AF demagnetized samples

steps.description: All magnetic remanence measurements were done such that samples were kept oriented in respect to each other.
steps.searchable_summary: maintain sample orientation during measurements

methods.description: Application of smoothing and correction procedures to tree ring density and magnetic susceptibility data.

steps.description: Both magnetic slope data and ring density data are approximated using a Stineman function.
steps.searchable_summary: approximate data using Stineman function

steps.description: The output of this function then has a geometric weight applied to the current point and ± 10% of the data range, to arrive at the smoothed curve.
steps.searchable_summary: apply geometric weighting to smoothed data

methods.description: Plotting measured and processed data as a function of age.

steps.description: Tree ring density (signal-to-noise ratio ~15/1) and both high-and low-field magnetic susceptibility (susceptibility record has sig-nal-to-noise ratio ~12/1 based on repetitive measurements) are plotted as a function of age Figure 2
steps.searchable_summary: plot tree ring density and magnetic susceptibility vs age

steps.description: Plot low temperature magnetic data (see Figure 3).
steps.searchable_summary: plot low temperature magnetic data

steps.description: Plot NRM and SIRM from sister samples ( Figure 4)
steps.searchable_summary: plot NRM and SIRM data

steps.description: Plot partially thermally demagnetized SIRM data ( Figure 5)
steps.searchable_summary: plot thermal demagnetization data

steps.description: Plot NRM magnetizations from all samples (see PPL data set, Figure 6).
steps.searchable_summary: plot NRM magnetization directions
